raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/XXXX I purchased XXXX airline tickets to XXXX ( for XX/XX/XXXX ) in the amount of {$890.00}. Within a few days ( XX/XX/XXXX ) I filed a dispute with Citibank because I felt that the company I bought the tickets from was a scam. Citibank opened the dispute and credited the money back to me. Citibank stated that they were having a problem contacting the merchant but that the merchant had until XXXX, XXXX to respond. The merchant responded and Citibank paid them back the {$890.00}. I called Citibank and they stated that I was to receive the tickets by the end of XXXX. I told Citibank that if I didn't get them I would call them back. I then called the merchant and was told the same thing. Citibank sent me a letter on XX/XX/XXXX asking if I wanted to continue with the dispute and to sign the letter and provide further documentation to support the dispute. At that time I couldn't dispute the charge because I was to receive the tickets in XXXX, XXXX. XXXX came and I didn't receive the tickets or the refund. I called the merchant and they told me to call Citibank. I called Citibank and they reopened my dispute. On XX/XX/XXXX I received a XXXX way call from Citibank ( maybe a scam ) and the merchant. The Citibank representative told me to do what the merchant wanted me to do if I wanted my refund. The merchant wanted me to download an app called XXXX. When I got to a certain point I said put my Citibank representative back on. I was told that they couldn't. I hung up and called Citibank. Citibank said that they never called and would never have me download an app. Here is it is XX/XX/XXXX and I have no tickets and no refund. Its been an ongoing dispute since XX/XX/XXXX.
